description: This PPG should be read in conjunction with APS 220 Credit Risk Management.
Under Attachment C of APS 220, ADIs must apply a buffer over a loan's interest rate of at least 3.0 per cent, unless determined otherwise by APRA.
APRA expects an ADI to have appropriate processes to determine whether loans approved through overrides would be treated as standard or non-standard loans for capital purposes under APS 112.
An ADI is required to have regard to the requirements governing security valuation practices, including valuation of security in the form of property, as detailed in Attachment A of APS 220.
Defaulted exposure is defined by reference to the non-performing exposure definition in APS 220.
Defaulted property exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E rather than Attachment A.
The loan amount for LVR purposes includes any undrawn committed amount as set out in Attachment C to this Prudential Standard.
Property valuations must be appraised independently using prudently conservative criteria consistent with the requirements set out in APS 220.
Unsettled and failed transactions must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment D to this Prudential Standard.
Defaulted exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E to this Prudential Standard.
Where Attachment B requires risk weights based on external credit ratings, ADIs must also comply with the requirements in Attachment F.
Residential and commercial property exposures in covered bond cover pools must meet the standard loan requirements in paragraphs 3 to 7 of Attachment A.
Bank assets in covered bond cover pools must qualify for a risk weight of 30 per cent or lower in accordance with paragraphs 8 to 10 of Attachment B.
Unrated or non-qualifying covered bonds must be treated as bank exposures and risk-weighted using Table 7 of Attachment B.
ADIs must apply Table 10 risk weights to rated general corporate exposures.
ADIs must apply Table 11 risk weights to short-term rated general corporate exposures.
Rated specialised lending exposures with an issue-specific credit rating must be risk-weighted using Table 10.
After applying CCFs, committed but undrawn amounts must be risk-weighted according to the requirements in Attachments A, B and D to APS 112.
ADIs must apply the relevant risk weight based on the counterparty as detailed in Attachment B when risk-weighting equity exposures.
Where a rated entity has guaranteed another entity's exposure, the guarantee may be recognised for risk-weighting purposes if the criteria in Attachment I are satisfied.

Attachment G references Attachment B for determining risk weights applicable to third-party banks holding cash collateral and for identifying sovereign securities eligible for zero per cent risk weight.
Cash-funded credit-linked notes issued by the ADI against banking book exposures that fulfil the criteria in Attachment J may be treated as cash collateralised transactions under the simple approach.
SFT netting treatment under the comprehensive approach requires the netting agreement to meet the requirements detailed in Attachment H to this Prudential Standard.

On-balance sheet netting for RWA calculation must apply the simple or comprehensive approach as set out in Attachment G; internal banking/trading book netting requires collateralised instruments to comply with eligible collateral criteria in Attachment G.
Currency mismatch haircut for guarantees must be adjusted for actual revaluation frequency in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G to APS 112.
Cash-funded credit linked notes satisfying minimum credit derivative requirements may be treated as cash-collateralised transactions under Attachment G to APS 112.
Currency mismatch haircut for credit derivatives must be adjusted for actual revaluation frequency in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G to APS 112.
Paragraph 46 refers to Attachment A for detailed requirements on collateral valuation policies and processes.
Paragraph 108 refers to Attachment B for the prescribed provisioning approach that APRA may permit or require ADIs to implement.
Attachment B references APS 112 for the definition of eligible lenders mortgage insurer and for the eligible collateral provisions relevant to cash on deposit deductions in prescribed provisioning.
